---
title: "Diagnostics spatiaux — Bassin SLSO"
author: "Essi"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
execute:
  warning: false
  echo: true
---

# Diagnostics spatiaux du modèle meandre

Ce notebook charge le modèle entraîné, simule en mode diagnostique (débit naturalisé, sans prélèvements), puis cartographie les champs hydrologiques distribués : ETR, recharge, ruissellement latéral, neige, etc.

## 1. Configuration et chargement

```{python}
#| label: setup
import os
os.chdir("C:/Users/parse01/documents-locaux/GitHub/meandre")
#os.chdir("/home/essi/Documents/GIT/meandre")

import tomllib
from pathlib import Path

import duckdb
import lets_plot as lp
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import torch
import xarray as xr

lp.LetsPlot.setup_html()

os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = "expandable_segments:True"

from meandre.data.basin_cache import BasinCache
from meandre.data.gridded_forcing import extract_forcing
from meandre.model import HydroModel
from meandre.routing.withdrawals import WithdrawalData
from meandre.utils.state import HydroState

#device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
device = torch.device("cpu") # empêcher le conflit avec le GPU en simulation
print(f"Device : {device}")
```

```{python}
#| label: load-config
# Charger la configuration
CONFIG = Path(f"notebooks/slso/config/slso.toml")
with open(CONFIG, "rb") as f:
    cfg = tomllib.load(f)

paths = cfg["paths"]
temporal = cfg["temporal"]
mcfg = cfg["model"]
```

```{python}
#| label: load-basin
# Charger les données du bassin
cache = BasinCache(paths["basin_db"])
hydro = cache.load(device=device)

graph = hydro["graph"]
territorial = hydro["territorial"]
node_coords = hydro["node_coords"]
n_nodes = hydro["n_nodes"]
node_ids = hydro["node_ids"]   # identifiants de tronçon Atlas, en ordre node_idx

print(f"Noeuds : {n_nodes}")
print(f"Arêtes : {graph.n_edges}")
print(f"Tronçons (ex.) : {node_ids[:5]}")
```

```{python}
#| label: load-forcing
# Période diagnostique : on utilise 2 ans de validation pour éviter les OOM
# (return_diagnostics=True conserve tous les champs en mémoire)
DIAG_START = "2010-01-01"
DIAG_END   = "2024-12-31"
DIAG_YEARS = f"{DIAG_START[:4]}–{DIAG_END[:4]}"

forcing = extract_forcing(
    zarr_path=paths["weather_grid"],
    node_coords=node_coords,
    node_elev=None,
    date_start=temporal["date_start"],
    date_end=temporal["date_end"],
    cache_nc=paths["forcing_cache"],
    device=device,
)

# Dates et jour de l'année
ds_time = xr.open_dataset(paths["forcing_cache"])
all_dates = ds_time.time.sel(
    time=slice(temporal["date_start"], temporal["date_end"])
).values
ds_time.close()

dates_pd = pd.DatetimeIndex(all_dates)
doy = torch.tensor(dates_pd.dayofyear.values, dtype=torch.long, device=device)

print(f"Forçage total : {forcing.shape} ({all_dates[0]} à {all_dates[-1]})")
```

```{python}
#| label: load-model
# Charger le modèle entraîné
model = HydroModel(
    n_nodes=n_nodes,
    n_territorial=territorial.n_features,
    n_forcing=mcfg["n_forcing"],
    context_window=mcfg["context_window"],
    residual_history=mcfg["residual_history"],
    max_travel_time=mcfg["max_travel_days"],
    use_temporal=True,
    use_residual=True,
    use_travel_time_attn=True,
    use_temperature=True,
    dropout=mcfg.get("dropout", 0.0),
    param_mode=mcfg.get("param_mode", "nerf"),
).to(device)

ckpt_path = Path(paths["checkpoint"])
model.load(str(ckpt_path))
model.eval()

n_params = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())
print(f"Checkpoint : {ckpt_path}")
print(f"Paramètres : {n_params:,}")
```

## 2. Simulation diagnostique

On simule avec zéro prélèvements (débit naturalisé) et `return_diagnostics=True` pour obtenir les champs spatialisés jour par jour.

```{python}
#| label: simulate
# Indices temporels
days = all_dates.astype("datetime64[D]")
diag_start_idx = int(np.searchsorted(days, np.datetime64(DIAG_START, "D")))
diag_end_idx = int(np.searchsorted(days, np.datetime64(DIAG_END, "D"), side="right"))

print(f"Période diagnostique : {DIAG_START} à {DIAG_END}")
print(f"  indices [{diag_start_idx}:{diag_end_idx}] sur {len(days)} jours total")

# Phase 1 : spinup (sans diagnostics) pour initialiser l'état
print("Spinup en cours...")
with torch.no_grad():
    Q_spinup, spinup_state = model.simulate(
        forcing=forcing[:diag_start_idx],
        initial_state=HydroState.zeros(n_nodes, device=device),
        graph=graph,
        node_coords=node_coords,
        territorial=territorial,
        withdrawals=WithdrawalData.zeros(diag_start_idx, n_nodes, device=device),
        day_of_year=doy[:diag_start_idx],
        return_diagnostics=False,
    )
del Q_spinup
torch.cuda.empty_cache() if device.type == "cuda" else None

# Phase 2 : simulation diagnostique
print("Simulation diagnostique en cours...")
with torch.no_grad():
    Q_sim, final_state, diag = model.simulate(
        forcing=forcing[diag_start_idx:diag_end_idx],
        initial_state=spinup_state,
        graph=graph,
        node_coords=node_coords,
        territorial=territorial,
        withdrawals=WithdrawalData.zeros(diag_end_idx - diag_start_idx, n_nodes, device=device),
        day_of_year=doy[diag_start_idx:diag_end_idx],
        return_diagnostics=True,
    )

diag_dates = pd.date_range(DIAG_START, DIAG_END, freq="D")
n_diag_days = Q_sim.shape[0]
print(f"Simulation terminée : {n_diag_days} jours, {n_nodes} noeuds")
print(f"Champs diagnostiques : {list(diag.to_dict().keys())}")
```

## 3. Bilan hydrique annuel

Bilan moyen annuel (mm/an) sur l'ensemble des noeuds.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-water-balance
#| tbl-cap: "Bilan hydrique annuel moyen (mm/an) — période diagnostique"

# Extraire les moyennes spatiales (mm/jour → mm/an × 365.25)
fields_mm = {
    "ETP": diag.etp,
    "ETR": diag.etr,
    "Fonte neige": diag.snowmelt,
    "Ruissellement lat.": diag.lateral_mm,
    "Recharge": diag.recharge,
    "Débit de base": diag.q_baseflow,
}

n_years = n_diag_days / 365.25
rows = []
for name, tensor in fields_mm.items():
    t = tensor.cpu().numpy()
    # Moyenne spatiale par jour, puis total annuel
    daily_mean = t.mean(axis=1)  # moyenne sur les noeuds
    total = float(daily_mean.sum() / n_years)
    spatial_std = float(t.sum(axis=0).mean() / n_years)  # variabilité inter-noeuds
    rows.append({"Variable": name, "Moyenne (mm/an)": f"{total:.0f}",
                 "Min noeud (mm/an)": f"{t.sum(axis=0).min() / n_years:.0f}",
                 "Max noeud (mm/an)": f"{t.sum(axis=0).max() / n_years:.0f}"})

df_balance = pd.DataFrame(rows)
print(df_balance)
```

## 4. Cartes spatiales

Cartes des champs moyens annuels sur le bassin SLSO.

```{python}
#| label: fig-spatial-maps
#| fig-cap: "Champs hydrologiques moyens annuels (mm/an) — SLSO"

lon = node_coords[:, 0].cpu().numpy()
lat = node_coords[:, 1].cpu().numpy()

# Calcul des moyennes annuelles par noeud (mm/jour → mm/an)
etr_annual = diag.etr.cpu().numpy().sum(axis=0) / n_years
recharge_annual = diag.recharge.cpu().numpy().sum(axis=0) / n_years
lateral_annual = diag.lateral_mm.cpu().numpy().sum(axis=0) / n_years
snowmelt_annual = diag.snowmelt.cpu().numpy().sum(axis=0) / n_years
baseflow_annual = diag.q_baseflow.cpu().numpy().sum(axis=0) / n_years
etp_annual = diag.etp.cpu().numpy().sum(axis=0) / n_years

fields = [
    ("ETR (mm/an)", etr_annual, "YlGn"),
    ("ETP (mm/an)", etp_annual, "YlOrRd"),
    ("Recharge (mm/an)", recharge_annual, "Blues"),
    ("Ruissellement latéral (mm/an)", lateral_annual, "PuBu"),
    ("Fonte de neige (mm/an)", snowmelt_annual, "cool"),
    ("Débit de base (mm/an)", baseflow_annual, "GnBu"),
]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(18, 11))
for ax, (title, values, cmap) in zip(axes.flat, fields):
    sc = ax.scatter(lon, lat, c=values, s=3, cmap=cmap, edgecolors="none")
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=12)
    ax.set_xlabel("Longitude")
    ax.set_ylabel("Latitude")
    ax.set_aspect("equal")
    plt.colorbar(sc, ax=ax, shrink=0.7)

fig.suptitle(f"Bassin SLSO — Moyennes annuelles {DIAG_YEARS}",
             fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-deficit-map
#| fig-cap: "Déficit hydrique (ETP - ETR) et ratio ETR/ETP"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5.5))

# Déficit
deficit = etp_annual - etr_annual
sc1 = axes[0].scatter(lon, lat, c=deficit, s=3, cmap="RdYlBu_r", edgecolors="none")
axes[0].set_title("Déficit hydrique ETP − ETR (mm/an)")
axes[0].set_xlabel("Longitude")
axes[0].set_ylabel("Latitude")
axes[0].set_aspect("equal")
plt.colorbar(sc1, ax=axes[0], shrink=0.7)

# Ratio ETR/ETP
ratio = np.where(etp_annual > 0, etr_annual / etp_annual, 0)
sc2 = axes[1].scatter(lon, lat, c=ratio, s=3, cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=0, vmax=1,
                       edgecolors="none")
axes[1].set_title("Ratio ETR / ETP (–)")
axes[1].set_xlabel("Longitude")
axes[1].set_ylabel("Latitude")
axes[1].set_aspect("equal")
plt.colorbar(sc2, ax=axes[1], shrink=0.7)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## 5. Séries temporelles

### Débit simulé à l'exutoire

```{python}
#| label: fig-discharge-outlet
#| fig-cap: "Débit simulé (naturalisé) à l'exutoire — SLSO"

# L'exutoire est le dernier noeud dans l'ordre topologique
outlet_idx = graph.topo_order[-1].item()

df_outlet = pd.DataFrame({
    "date": diag_dates[:n_diag_days],
    "Q":    Q_sim[:, outlet_idx].cpu().numpy(),
})

(lp.ggplot(df_outlet, lp.aes("date", "Q"))
 + lp.geom_line(color="black", size=0.6)
 + lp.labs(x="Date", y="Débit (m³/s)",
           title=f"Débit naturalisé à l'exutoire — tronçon {node_ids[outlet_idx]} ({DIAG_YEARS})")
 + lp.theme_bw()
 + lp.ggsize(1300, 320)
)
```

### Moyennes spatiales des flux verticaux

```{python}
#| label: fig-timeseries-fluxes
#| fig-cap: "Séries temporelles des flux verticaux et température moyens sur le bassin"

_dates = diag_dates[:n_diag_days]
_d = diag

# Palette et ordre des panneaux (flux en mm/jour + température en °C)
_panels = [
    ("ETP (mm/jour)",              _d.etp.cpu().numpy().mean(axis=1)),
    ("ETR (mm/jour)",              _d.etr.cpu().numpy().mean(axis=1)),
    ("Fonte de neige (mm/jour)",   _d.snowmelt.cpu().numpy().mean(axis=1)),
    ("Ruissellement (mm/jour)",    _d.lateral_mm.cpu().numpy().mean(axis=1)),
    ("Recharge (mm/jour)",         _d.recharge.cpu().numpy().mean(axis=1)),
    ("Débit de base (mm/jour)",    _d.q_baseflow.cpu().numpy().mean(axis=1)),
]
if _d.T_water is not None:
    _panels.append(
        ("T eau (°C)", _d.T_water.cpu().numpy().mean(axis=1)),
    )

_rows = []
for label, values in _panels:
    for d, v in zip(_dates, values):
        _rows.append({"date": d, "valeur": v, "variable": label})
df_fluxes = pd.DataFrame(_rows)

(lp.ggplot(df_fluxes, lp.aes("date", "valeur"))
 + lp.geom_line(size=0.5, color="black")
 + lp.facet_wrap("variable", ncol=3, scales="free_y")
 + lp.labs(x="Date", y="",
           title="Flux verticaux et température — moyenne spatiale sur le bassin SLSO")
 + lp.theme_bw()
 + lp.theme(strip_text=lp.element_text(size=10))
 + lp.ggsize(2000, 1400)
)
```

## 6. Influence des prélèvements et rejets

Comparaison débit naturalisé vs débit géré (avec prélèvements/rejets) pour
identifier les tronçons les plus et les moins affectés.

```{python}
#| label: simulate-managed
# Charger les prélèvements/rejets réels depuis le DuckDB
withdrawals_real = cache.load_withdrawals(
    date_start=DIAG_START,
    date_end=DIAG_END,
    device=device,
)

w_total = withdrawals_real.net.abs().sum().item()
print(f"Signal prélèvements/rejets : {w_total:.1f} m³/s·jour cumulé")
if w_total == 0:
    print("⚠ Aucun prélèvement/rejet dans la DB — les deux simulations seront identiques.")

print("Simulation gérée en cours...")
with torch.no_grad():
    Q_managed, *_ = model.simulate(
        forcing=forcing[diag_start_idx:diag_end_idx],
        initial_state=spinup_state,
        graph=graph,
        node_coords=node_coords,
        territorial=territorial,
        withdrawals=withdrawals_real,
        day_of_year=doy[diag_start_idx:diag_end_idx],
        return_diagnostics=False,
    )

q_nat     = Q_sim.cpu().numpy()           # (T, N)
q_managed = Q_managed.cpu().numpy()       # (T, N)
delta     = q_managed - q_nat             # positif = rejet net, négatif = prélèvement net
```

### Comparaison naturalisé vs géré à l'exutoire

```{python}
#| label: fig-nat-vs-managed
#| fig-cap: "Débit naturalisé vs géré à l'exutoire"

_dates_out = diag_dates[:n_diag_days]
df_cmp = pd.concat([
    pd.DataFrame({"date": _dates_out, "Q": q_nat[:, outlet_idx],     "série": "Naturalisé"}),
    pd.DataFrame({"date": _dates_out, "Q": q_managed[:, outlet_idx], "série": "Géré"}),
], ignore_index=True)

(lp.ggplot(df_cmp, lp.aes("date", "Q", color="série"))
 + lp.geom_line(size=0.5, alpha=0.8)
 + lp.scale_color_manual(values=["#2166ac", "#d6604d"], name="")
 + lp.labs(x="Date", y="Débit (m³/s)",
           title=f"Débit naturalisé vs géré — exutoire tronçon {node_ids[outlet_idx]} ({DIAG_YEARS})")
 + lp.theme_bw()
 + lp.ggsize(1300, 320)
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-withdrawal-impact-map
#| fig-cap: "Influence des prélèvements/rejets par tronçon"

# Métrique d'impact
delta_amp   = np.abs(delta).mean(axis=0)                              # (N,) amplitude moyenne (m³/s)
q_nat_mean  = np.maximum(q_nat.mean(axis=0), 1e-6)                    # (N,) débit naturalisé moyen
proportion  = np.clip(delta_amp / q_nat_mean, 0, None)                # (N,) proportion sans borne sup

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 6))

amp_min = delta_amp[delta_amp > 0].min() if (delta_amp > 0).any() else 1e-6
sc1 = axes[0].scatter(lon, lat, c=delta_amp, s=4,
                       cmap="OrRd", edgecolors="none",
                       norm=plt.matplotlib.colors.LogNorm(vmin=amp_min, vmax=delta_amp.max()))
axes[0].set_title("Amplitude moyenne |ΔQ| (m³/s)\n(échelle log)")
axes[0].set_aspect("equal")
plt.colorbar(sc1, ax=axes[0], shrink=0.7)

prop_max = np.percentile(proportion[proportion > 0], 99) if (proportion > 0).any() else 1.0
from matplotlib.colors import LogNorm
sc2 = axes[1].scatter(lon, lat, c=proportion.clip(1e-6), s=4,
                       cmap="YlOrRd", edgecolors="none",
                       norm=LogNorm(vmin=1e-4, vmax=max(prop_max, 1e-2)))
axes[1].set_title("Proportion d'influence annuelle |ΔQ| / Q_nat\n(impact anthropique / débit naturalisé)")
axes[1].set_aspect("equal")
plt.colorbar(sc2, ax=axes[1], shrink=0.7)

fig.suptitle(f"Influence des prélèvements/rejets — SLSO {DIAG_YEARS}",
             fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-most-impacted
#| fig-cap: "12 tronçons les plus affectés — naturalisé vs géré"


N_TOP = 12
rank         = np.argsort(delta_amp)[::-1]
top_nodes    = rank[:N_TOP]
bottom_nodes = rank[-N_TOP:]

def _reach_df(nodes, q_nat, q_managed, dates, lon, lat, node_ids):
    """Construit un DataFrame long (naturalisé + géré) pour les noeuds donnés."""
    rows = []
    for node in nodes:
        qn   = q_nat[:, node]
        qm   = q_managed[:, node]
        damp = float(np.abs(qm - qn).mean())
        prop = damp / max(float(qn.mean()), 1e-6)
        tid  = node_ids[node]
        label = f"{tid}  |ΔQ|={damp:.4f}  |ΔQ|/Q={prop:.3f}"
        for d, vn, vm in zip(dates, qn, qm):
            rows.append({"date": d, "Q": vn, "type": "Naturalisé", "troncon": label})
            rows.append({"date": d, "Q": vm, "type": "Géré",        "troncon": label})
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)

def _plot_reach_lp(nodes, title, q_nat, q_managed, dates, lon, lat, node_ids):
    df = _reach_df(nodes, q_nat, q_managed, dates, lon, lat, node_ids)
    # Categorical ordonné = lets_plot respecte l'ordre des facettes
    ordered_labels = [df.loc[df["troncon"].str.startswith(f"{node_ids[n]} "), "troncon"].iloc[0]
                      for n in nodes]
    df["troncon"] = pd.Categorical(df["troncon"], categories=ordered_labels, ordered=True)
    return (
        lp.ggplot(df, lp.aes("date", "Q", color="type"))
        + lp.geom_line(size=0.6, alpha=0.85)
        + lp.facet_wrap("troncon", ncol=4, scales="free_y")
        + lp.labs(x="Date", y="m³/s", title=title)
        + lp.theme_bw()
        + lp.theme(legend_position="top",
                   strip_text=lp.element_text(size=8),
                   axis_text_x=lp.element_text(size=7))
        + lp.ggsize(1500, 1000)
    )
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-most-impacted
#| fig-cap: "10 tronçons les plus affectés — naturalisé vs géré"

_plot_reach_lp(
    top_nodes,
    f"{N_TOP} tronçons les PLUS affectés par les prélèvements/rejets",
    q_nat, q_managed, diag_dates[:n_diag_days], lon, lat, node_ids,
)
```


```{python}
#| label: tbl-impact-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Résumé de l'impact par tronçon (top 10 + bottom 10)"

rows_impact = []
for node in list(top_nodes) + list(bottom_nodes):
    qn   = q_nat[:, node]
    qm   = q_managed[:, node]
    damp = float(np.abs(qm - qn).mean())
    prop = damp / max(float(qn.mean()), 1e-6)
    rows_impact.append({
        "Tronçon": node_ids[node],
        "Lon": f"{lon[node]:.3f}",
        "Lat": f"{lat[node]:.3f}",
        "|ΔQ| moy (m³/s)": f"{damp:.4f}",
        "|ΔQ|/Q_nat (–)": f"{prop:.4f}",
        "ΔQ max (m³/s)": f"{(qm - qn).max():+.4f}",
        "ΔQ min (m³/s)": f"{(qm - qn).min():+.4f}",
        "Groupe": "top 10" if node in top_nodes else "bottom 10",
    })

df_impact = pd.DataFrame(rows_impact)
print(df_impact.to_string(index=False))
```

### Analyse signal/bruit (SNR) des prélèvements

Le ratio signal/bruit (SNR) mesure l'amplitude des prélèvements par rapport au débit naturel pour chaque tronçon affecté — directement ou via un site en amont.

```{python}
#| label: snr-analysis

# Identifier les nœuds avec prélèvements directs (depuis le DuckDB)
import duckdb as _ddb
_con = _ddb.connect(paths["basin_db"], read_only=True)
wd_nodes = set(_con.execute("SELECT DISTINCT node_idx FROM withdrawals").df()["node_idx"])
_con.close()

# Remonter le graphe: trouver les nœuds en aval des prélèvements
edge_src = graph.edge_index[0].numpy() if graph.n_edges > 0 else np.array([], dtype=int)
edge_dst = graph.edge_index[1].numpy() if graph.n_edges > 0 else np.array([], dtype=int)

# Construire l'adjacence aval (src → dst)
from collections import defaultdict, deque
downstream = defaultdict(list)
for s, d in zip(edge_src, edge_dst):
    downstream[int(s)].append(int(d))

# Propager en aval: tout nœud en aval d'un prélèvement est affecté
affected = set(wd_nodes)
queue = deque(wd_nodes)
while queue:
    node = queue.popleft()
    for child in downstream[node]:
        if child not in affected:
            affected.add(child)
            queue.append(child)

n_direct = len(wd_nodes)
n_affected = len(affected)
print(f"Tronçons avec prélèvement direct : {n_direct}")
print(f"Tronçons affectés (direct + aval) : {n_affected}")

# Calcul du SNR par tronçon affecté
snr = {}
for node in affected:
    q_mean = max(float(q_nat[:, node].mean()), 1e-8)
    dq_mean = float(np.abs(delta[:, node]).mean())
    snr[node] = dq_mean / q_mean

snr_pct = np.array(list(snr.values())) * 100  # en %

n_gt_1   = int((snr_pct > 1).sum())
n_gt_10  = int((snr_pct > 10).sum())
n_gt_100 = int((snr_pct > 100).sum())

print(f"\n{'─' * 50}")
print(f"Analyse SNR sur {n_affected} tronçons affectés")
print(f"{'─' * 50}")
print(f"  SNR > 1%   : {n_gt_1:4d} tronçons ({100*n_gt_1/n_affected:.1f}%)")
print(f"  SNR > 10%  : {n_gt_10:4d} tronçons ({100*n_gt_10/n_affected:.1f}%)")
print(f"  SNR > 100% : {n_gt_100:4d} tronçons ({100*n_gt_100/n_affected:.1f}%)")
print(f"\n  Médiane SNR : {float(np.median(snr_pct)):.2f}%")
print(f"  Moyenne SNR : {float(np.mean(snr_pct)):.2f}%")
print(f"  Max SNR     : {float(np.max(snr_pct)):.1f}% (tronçon {node_ids[max(snr, key=snr.get)]})")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-snr-histogram
#| fig-cap: "Distribution du SNR (|ΔQ|/Q_nat) sur les tronçons affectés"

df_snr = pd.DataFrame({
    "troncon": [node_ids[n] for n in snr],
    "SNR_pct": [snr[n] * 100 for n in snr],
})

(lp.ggplot(df_snr, lp.aes("SNR_pct"))
 + lp.geom_histogram(fill="steelblue", color="black", bins=50)
 + lp.scale_x_log10()
 + lp.geom_vline(xintercept=1, color="orange", linetype="dashed", size=0.8)
 + lp.geom_vline(xintercept=10, color="red", linetype="dashed", size=0.8)
 + lp.labs(x="SNR (%)", y="Nombre de tronçons",
           title=f"Distribution du ratio signal/bruit — {n_affected} tronçons affectés")
 + lp.theme_bw()
 + lp.ggsize(900, 400)
)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-snr-map
#| fig-cap: "Carte spatiale du SNR par tronçon affecté"

from matplotlib.colors import LogNorm

# SNR pour tous les nœuds (0 pour les non-affectés)
snr_all = np.zeros(len(node_ids))
for node, val in snr.items():
    snr_all[node] = val * 100  # en %

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 8))

# Arêtes du réseau complet en gris très pâle (structure hydrographique)
from matplotlib.collections import LineCollection
segments = [[(lon[s], lat[s]), (lon[d], lat[d])]
            for s, d in zip(edge_src, edge_dst)
            if int(d) != outlet_idx]
lc_bg = LineCollection(segments, colors="whitesmoke", linewidths=0.3, zorder=1)
ax.add_collection(lc_bg)

# Arêtes affectées colorées par SNR aval
affected_set = set(snr.keys())
aff_segs, aff_colors = [], []
for s, d in zip(edge_src, edge_dst):
    si, di = int(s), int(d)
    if di in affected_set and di != outlet_idx:
        aff_segs.append([(lon[si], lat[si]), (lon[di], lat[di])])
        aff_colors.append(snr_all[di])

aff_colors = np.array(aff_colors).clip(1e-3)
lc = LineCollection(aff_segs, cmap="YlOrRd",
                    norm=LogNorm(vmin=0.01, vmax=max(snr_all.max(), 10)),
                    linewidths=0.8, zorder=2)
lc.set_array(aff_colors)
ax.add_collection(lc)

cb = plt.colorbar(lc, ax=ax, shrink=0.7)
cb.set_label("SNR (%)")
ax.set_xlabel("Longitude")
ax.set_ylabel("Latitude")
ax.set_title(f"Propagation aval des prélèvements — {n_affected} tronçons affectés sur {len(node_ids)}")
ax.set_aspect("equal")
ax.autoscale_view()
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Signal anthropique vs variabilité naturelle

Comparaison de l'amplitude du signal anthropique |ΔQ| à la variabilité naturelle σ(Q_nat) pour chaque tronçon affecté, avec la distribution du SNR et sa répartition spatiale.

```{python}
#| label: fig-signal-vs-noise
#| fig-cap: "Signal anthropique vs variabilité naturelle — SLSO"

from matplotlib.colors import LogNorm
from matplotlib.ticker import LogLocator, NullFormatter

# --- Calculs ---
# Variabilité naturelle = écart-type temporel du débit naturalisé par tronçon
sigma_nat = q_nat.std(axis=0)          # (N,)
delta_abs_mean = np.abs(delta).mean(axis=0)  # (N,) signal moyen |ΔQ|

# SNR = |ΔQ| / σ(Q_nat)
snr_sigma = np.where(sigma_nat > 0, delta_abs_mean / sigma_nat, 0.0)

# Filtrer les tronçons affectés
aff_idx = np.array(sorted(affected))
sig_aff    = delta_abs_mean[aff_idx]
sigma_aff  = sigma_nat[aff_idx]
snr_aff    = snr_sigma[aff_idx]
lon_aff    = lon[aff_idx]
lat_aff    = lat[aff_idx]

# Masque pour valeurs positives (log-log)
pos_mask = (sig_aff > 0) & (sigma_aff > 0) & (snr_aff > 0)

# --- Figure 3 panneaux ---
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(20, 6.5),
                          gridspec_kw={"width_ratios": [1, 1, 1.15]})

# ── Panneau 1 : Signal vs bruit (log-log) ──
ax1 = axes[0]
sc1 = ax1.scatter(
    sigma_aff[pos_mask], sig_aff[pos_mask],
    c=snr_aff[pos_mask], s=8,
    cmap="magma", norm=LogNorm(vmin=1e-4, vmax=1e2),
    edgecolors="none", alpha=0.7,
)
# Lignes de référence
xlims = (1e-4, 1e2)
ax1.plot(xlims, xlims, "k--", lw=0.8, label="1:1 (signal = bruit)")
ax1.plot(xlims, [x * 0.1 for x in xlims], "-", color="silver", lw=0.8, label="10% du bruit")
ax1.set_xscale("log"); ax1.set_yscale("log")
ax1.set_xlim(xlims); ax1.set_ylim(1e-12, 1e2)
ax1.set_xlabel(r"$\sigma(Q_{nat})$ — variabilité naturelle (m³/s)")
ax1.set_ylabel(r"|ΔQ| — signal anthropique (m³/s)")
ax1.set_title("Signal vs bruit (log-log)")
ax1.legend(fontsize=8, loc="upper left")
cb1 = plt.colorbar(sc1, ax=ax1, shrink=0.8)
cb1.set_label("SNR (–)")

# ── Panneau 2 : Histogramme de log10(SNR) ──
ax2 = axes[1]
log_snr = np.log10(snr_aff[pos_mask])
ax2.hist(log_snr, bins=50, color="indianred", edgecolor="darkred", alpha=0.85)
# Lignes seuils
ax2.axvline(np.log10(0.01), color="gray", ls="--", lw=1, label="SNR = 1%")
ax2.axvline(np.log10(0.10), color="orange", ls="--", lw=1, label="SNR = 10%")
ax2.axvline(np.log10(1.00), color="red", ls="--", lw=1, label="SNR = 100%")
ax2.set_xlabel(r"$\log_{10}$(SNR)")
ax2.set_ylabel("Nombre de tronçons")
ax2.set_title("Distribution du rapport signal/bruit")
ax2.legend(fontsize=8)

# ── Panneau 3 : Carte spatiale SNR ──
ax3 = axes[2]
# Fond : tous les tronçons en gris clair
ax3.scatter(lon, lat, c="lightgrey", s=2, edgecolors="none", zorder=1)
# Tronçons affectés colorés par SNR
sc3 = ax3.scatter(
    lon_aff[pos_mask], lat_aff[pos_mask],
    c=snr_aff[pos_mask], s=6,
    cmap="magma", norm=LogNorm(vmin=1e-4, vmax=1e2),
    edgecolors="none", alpha=0.8, zorder=2,
)
ax3.set_xlabel("Longitude"); ax3.set_ylabel("Latitude")
ax3.set_title(f"SNR spatial — {pos_mask.sum()} tronçons affectés")
ax3.set_aspect("equal")
cb3 = plt.colorbar(sc3, ax=ax3, shrink=0.8)
cb3.set_label("SNR (–)")

fig.suptitle(f"Signal anthropique vs variabilité naturelle — SLSO {DIAG_YEARS}",
             fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## 7. Performance aux stations (KGE / NSE)

Chargement des observations de débit aux stations de jaugeage et calcul du KGE par station sur la période diagnostique, en comparant le débit naturalisé et le débit géré.

```{python}
#| label: fig-kge-map
#| fig-cap: "KGE par station de jaugeage — SLSO"

from meandre.utils.metrics import kge, nse

# Charger les observations sur la période diagnostique
obs = cache.load_observations(
    date_start=DIAG_START,
    date_end=DIAG_END,
    min_valid_days=30,
)

station_node_map = obs["station_node_map"]  # {station_id: node_index}
station_indices = sorted(set(station_node_map.values()))
discharge_np = obs["discharge"]  # (T_diag, N_all)
q_obs_diag = torch.from_numpy(discharge_np[:, station_indices]).to(device).cpu()

obs_dates = obs["dates"]
sids = list(station_node_map.keys())

print(f"Obs shape: {q_obs_diag.shape}, Q_sim shape: {Q_sim.shape}")
print(f"Stations: {len(sids)}")

import duckdb as _ddb
_con = _ddb.connect(paths["basin_db"], read_only=True)
sta_lons, sta_lats, sta_areas = [], [], []
for s in sids:
    row = _con.execute(
        "SELECT lon, lat, drainage_area_km2 FROM stations WHERE station_id = ?", [s]
    ).fetchone()
    sta_lons.append(float(row[0]) if row else 0.0)
    sta_lats.append(float(row[1]) if row else 0.0)
    sta_areas.append(float(row[2]) if row else 0.0)
_con.close()

# Extraire Q aux stations
station_mask_diag = torch.zeros(n_nodes, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
for ni in station_indices:
    station_mask_diag[ni] = True

q_sim_stations = Q_sim[:, station_mask_diag].cpu()
q_managed_stations = Q_managed[:, station_mask_diag].cpu()

# Aligner temporellement
n_common = min(q_sim_stations.shape[0], q_obs_diag.shape[0])
q_obs_aligned = q_obs_diag[:n_common, :]
q_sim_aligned = q_sim_stations[:n_common, :]
q_managed_aligned = q_managed_stations[:n_common, :]

# Calcul du KGE et NSE par station (naturalisé + géré)
kge_results = []
for i, sid in enumerate(sids):
    ni = station_node_map[sid]
    col = station_indices.index(ni)
    q_o = q_obs_aligned[:, col]
    q_s = q_sim_aligned[:, col]
    q_m = q_managed_aligned[:, col]
    valid = ~torch.isnan(q_o) & ~torch.isnan(q_s)
    if valid.sum() < 30:
        continue
    q_o_v, q_s_v, q_m_v = q_o[valid], q_s[valid], q_m[valid]
    from meandre.utils.metrics import kge_components as _kgec
    _kc = _kgec(q_o_v, q_s_v)
    kge_results.append({
        "station": sid,
        "node": ni,
        "lon": sta_lons[i],
        "lat": sta_lats[i],
        "area_km2": sta_areas[i],
        "mean_obs": float(q_o_v.mean()),
        "mean_sim": float(q_s_v.mean()),
        "r": float(_kc["r"]),
        "beta": float(_kc["beta"]),
        "gamma": float(_kc["gamma"]),
        "KGE_nat": float(kge(q_o_v, q_s_v)),
        "NSE_nat": float(nse(q_o_v, q_s_v)),
        "KGE_managed": float(kge(q_o_v, q_m_v)),
        "NSE_managed": float(nse(q_o_v, q_m_v)),
        "n_days": int(valid.sum()),
    })

df_kge = pd.DataFrame(kge_results)
print(f"Stations évaluées : {len(df_kge)}")
if len(df_kge) > 0:
    # KGE pondéré par sqrt(aire) comme dans l'entraînement
    w = np.sqrt(df_kge["area_km2"].clip(lower=50, upper=500).values)
    w = w / w.sum()

    print(f"\n── Débit naturalisé ──")
    print(f"KGE médian  : {df_kge['KGE_nat'].median():.3f}")
    print(f"KGE moyen   : {df_kge['KGE_nat'].mean():.3f}")
    print(f"KGE pondéré (√aire) : {(df_kge['KGE_nat'].values * w).sum():.3f}")

    print(f"\n── Débit géré (avec prélèvements) ──")
    print(f"KGE médian  : {df_kge['KGE_managed'].median():.3f}")
    print(f"KGE moyen   : {df_kge['KGE_managed'].mean():.3f}")
    print(f"KGE pondéré (√aire) : {(df_kge['KGE_managed'].values * w).sum():.3f}")

    print(f"\n── Diagnostic : mean_obs vs mean_sim + KGE components ──")
    print(df_kge[["station", "area_km2", "node", "mean_obs", "mean_sim", "r", "beta", "gamma", "KGE_nat", "NSE_nat", "n_days"]]
          .sort_values("area_km2", ascending=False)
          .to_string(index=False, float_format="{:.3f}".format))
else:
    print("Aucune station avec assez de données valides.")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-kge-spatial
#| fig-cap: "Carte spatiale des KGE aux stations de jaugeage"

assert len(df_kge) > 0, "Aucune station évaluée"

_sizes = df_kge["area_km2"].clip(upper=500) * 0.15 + 15

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 6))

# Carte KGE géré
sc1 = axes[0].scatter(
    df_kge["lon"], df_kge["lat"],
    c=df_kge["KGE_managed"], s=_sizes,
    cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=-0.5, vmax=1.0,
    edgecolors="black", linewidths=0.5, zorder=3,
)
axes[0].scatter(lon, lat, c="lightgrey", s=1, edgecolors="none", zorder=1)
axes[0].set_title(f"KGE (débit géré) — {DIAG_YEARS}")
axes[0].set_xlabel("Longitude")
axes[0].set_ylabel("Latitude")
axes[0].set_aspect("equal")
cb1 = plt.colorbar(sc1, ax=axes[0], shrink=0.7)
cb1.set_label("KGE (–)")

# Carte KGE naturalisé
sc2 = axes[1].scatter(
    df_kge["lon"], df_kge["lat"],
    c=df_kge["KGE_nat"], s=_sizes,
    cmap="RdYlGn", vmin=-0.5, vmax=1.0,
    edgecolors="black", linewidths=0.5, zorder=3,
)
axes[1].scatter(lon, lat, c="lightgrey", s=1, edgecolors="none", zorder=1)
axes[1].set_title(f"KGE (débit naturalisé) — {DIAG_YEARS}")
axes[1].set_xlabel("Longitude")
axes[1].set_ylabel("Latitude")
axes[1].set_aspect("equal")
cb2 = plt.colorbar(sc2, ax=axes[1], shrink=0.7)
cb2.set_label("KGE (–)")

fig.suptitle(f"Performance du modèle aux stations — SLSO {DIAG_YEARS}",
             fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## 8. Export NetCDF

Sauvegarde de tous les champs diagnostiques dans un fichier NetCDF pour
analyses ultérieures (SIG, comparaison inter-modèles, etc.).

```{python}
#| label: export-netcdf

# Construire le dataset xarray
coords_dict = {
    "time": diag_dates[:n_diag_days],
    "node": np.array(node_ids),
}

data_vars = {}

# Débit simulé
data_vars["Q_sim"] = (["time", "node"], Q_sim.cpu().numpy(), {
    "units": "m3/s",
    "long_name": "Débit simulé naturalisé",
})

# Champs diagnostiques
long_names = {
    "etp": "Évapotranspiration potentielle",
    "etr": "Évapotranspiration réelle",
    "snowmelt": "Fonte de neige",
    "lateral_mm": "Ruissellement latéral",
    "recharge": "Recharge de la nappe",
    "q_baseflow": "Débit de base",
    "q_lateral": "Apport latéral",
    "q_upstream": "Apport amont",
    "T_water": "Température de l'eau",
}

for name, tensor in diag.to_dict().items():
    data_vars[name] = (
        ["time", "node"],
        tensor.cpu().numpy(),
        {"units": diag.units[name], "long_name": long_names.get(name, name)},
    )

# Coordonnées géographiques des noeuds
data_vars["lon"] = (["node"], lon, {"units": "degrees_east", "long_name": "Longitude"})
data_vars["lat"] = (["node"], lat, {"units": "degrees_north", "long_name": "Latitude"})

ds = xr.Dataset(data_vars=data_vars, coords=coords_dict)
ds.attrs["title"] = "Diagnostics spatiaux HydroModel — Bassin SLSO"
ds.attrs["source"] = f"meandre, checkpoint: {ckpt_path.name}"
ds.attrs["period"] = f"{DIAG_START} à {DIAG_END}"
ds.attrs["mode"] = "Débit naturalisé (zéro prélèvements)"
ds.attrs["history"] = f"Créé le {pd.Timestamp.now().isoformat()}"

# Sauvegarder
output_nc = Path("notebooks/slso/results/diagnostics.nc")
output_nc.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
ds.to_netcdf(output_nc, engine="netcdf4")
print(f"NetCDF exporté : {output_nc} ({output_nc.stat().st_size / 1e6:.1f} Mo)")
print(f"Variables : {list(ds.data_vars)}")
print(f"Dimensions : time={ds.sizes['time']}, node={ds.sizes['node']}")
ds
```

```{python}
#| label: summary
print("=== Diagnostics terminés ===")
print(f"  Période       : {DIAG_START} à {DIAG_END}")
print(f"  Noeuds        : {n_nodes}")
print(f"  Pas de temps  : {n_diag_days}")
print(f"  Export         : {output_nc}")
print(f"  Tronçons affectés (|ΔQ|>0.01 m³/s) : {(np.abs(delta).mean(axis=0) > 0.01).sum()}/{n_nodes}")
```


```{python}

```
